ucs.title: [LLM] EXOTIC LILY initial access: spoofed-sender phish with legitimate file-share link followed by ISO/IMG/LNK execution
ucs.description: Hunts EXOTIC LILY's signature initial-access chain: spoofed/typosquatted senders impersonating partner firms send mail with links to legitimate file-sharing services (WeTransfer, TransferNow, OneDrive, MS OneDrive, Mediafire) that deliver an ISO/IMG containing an LNK + DLL BumbleBee loader. We correlate the click on the file-share link with a subsequent ISO/IMG mount and rundll32 spawn on the same recipient's host within minutes.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as click_time from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.url IN ("*wetransfer.com*","*transfernow.net*","*mediafire.com*","*1drv.ms*","*onedrive.live.com*","*share-eu1.hsforms.com*","*sendspace.com*") by Web.src, Web.user, Web.url, Web.dest
| rename Web.* as *
| eval user_lower=lower(user)
| join type=inner user_lower [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as file_time values(Filesystem.file_name) as iso_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.iso" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.img" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vhd" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk") by Filesystem.user, Filesystem.dest, Filesystem.file_path
    | rename Filesystem.* as *
    | eval user_lower=lower(user) ]
| where file_time >= click_time AND file_time <= click_time + 1800
| join type=inner dest user_lower [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as cmd min(_time) as proc_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","odbcconf.exe") AND (Processes.process="*\\\\*.dll*" OR Processes.process="*,*") by Processes.user, Processes.dest, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.process_name
    | rename Processes.* as *
    | eval user_lower=lower(user) ]
| where proc_time >= file_time AND proc_time <= file_time + 600
| table click_time, file_time, proc_time, user, dest, url, iso_files, file_path, parent_process_name, process_name, cmd
| sort - click_time
ucs.kql:
// EXOTIC LILY (G1011) — file-share phish link → ISO/IMG mount → rundll32 BumbleBee loader
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ClickToFileMin = 30;     // analyst window: click → ISO/IMG written
let FileToProcMin  = 10;     // ISO mount → rundll32 spawn
let FileShareDomains = dynamic([
    "wetransfer.com","we.tl","transfernow.net","mediafire.com",
    "1drv.ms","onedrive.live.com","sendspace.com","disk.yandex.com",
    "smash.com","sharefile.com","hightail.com","filemail.com"]);
// 1. Inbound mail with a link to a legitimate file-share service —
//    EXOTIC LILY hides behind these because URL reputation is clean.
let PhishMail = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound" and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailUrlInfo | project NetworkMessageId, Url, UrlDomain
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | where UrlDomain has_any (FileShareDomains)
    | project NetworkMessageId, EmailTime = Timestamp,
              SenderFromAddress, SenderMailFromDomain,
              RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, Url, UrlDomain;
// 2. Recipient actually clicked through Safe Links to that file-share link.
let PhishClicks = PhishMail
    | join kind=inner (
        UrlClickEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
        | where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
        | project NetworkMessageId, ClickTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn
      ) on NetworkMessageId;
// 3. ISO / IMG / VHD container written to disk on the recipient's device
//    shortly after the click — this is the BumbleBee delivery vehicle.
let IsoDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".iso" or FileName endswith ".img"
         or FileName endswith ".vhd" or FileName endswith ".vhdx"
    | project FileTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              IsoName = FileName, IsoPath = FolderPath,
              FileWriter = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              AccountUpn = InitiatingProcessAccountUpn;
let ClickAndDrop = PhishClicks
    | join kind=inner IsoDrops on AccountUpn
    | where FileTime between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + ClickToFileMin * 1m);
// 4. rundll32 / regsvr32 / odbcconf launched within minutes — BumbleBee
//    is a DLL whose export is invoked via rundll32 from the mounted ISO.
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","odbcconf.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".dll",",")
| join kind=inner ClickAndDrop on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (FileTime .. FileTime + FileToProcMin * 1m)
| project ClickTime, FileTime, ProcTime = Timestamp,
          DeviceName, AccountUpn, SenderFromAddress, SenderMailFromDomain,
          Subject, Url, UrlDomain,
          IsoName, IsoPath,
          Loader = FileName, LoaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          DelaySecClickToProc = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ClickTime)
| order by ClickTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// EXOTIC LILY — file-share phish link → ISO mount → rundll32 BumbleBee loader
// Sentinel relies on OfficeActivity (no UrlClickEvents equivalent), so we
// pivot from inbound message metadata directly to ISO file write +
// rundll32 process create on the recipient host within 30 minutes.
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30;
let FileShareDomains = dynamic([
    "wetransfer.com","we.tl","transfernow.net","mediafire.com",
    "1drv.ms","onedrive.live.com","sendspace.com","disk.yandex.com",
    "smash.com","sharefile.com","hightail.com","filemail.com"]);
let PhishedUsers = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("Receive","Deliver")
    | extend Recipients = tostring(Recipients), Sender = tostring(Sender)
    | where Recipients has_any (FileShareDomains) or Subject has_any (FileShareDomains)
    | project EmailTime = TimeGenerated, Sender, Recipients,
              Subject, MailboxOwnerUPN;
let IsoDrops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".iso" or TargetFileName endswith ".img"
         or TargetFileName endswith ".vhd" or TargetFileName endswith ".vhdx"
    | project FileTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              IsoName = TargetFileName, IsoPath = TargetFilePath,
              ActorUsername;
let RunDll = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith "\\regsvr32.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith "\\odbcconf.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any (".dll",",")
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              Loader = NewProcessName, LoaderCmd = CommandLine,
              Parent = ParentProcessName;
IsoDrops
| join kind=inner RunDll on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer
| where ProcTime between (FileTime .. FileTime + WindowMin * 1m)
| join kind=inner PhishedUsers on $left.ActorUsername == $right.MailboxOwnerUPN
| where FileTime between (EmailTime .. EmailTime + WindowMin * 1m)
| project EmailTime, FileTime, ProcTime,
          Computer = DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          Sender, Subject, Recipients,
          IsoName, IsoPath, Loader, LoaderCmd, Parent,
          DelaySecMailToProc = datetime_diff('second', ProcTime, EmailTime)
| order by EmailTime desc
ucs.rationale: EXOTIC LILY's documented playbook (Google TAG, Mar 2022) is unique among IABs: typosquatted/spoofed sender personas + a *legitimate* file-share service link to bypass URL reputation, delivering an ISO/IMG containing a LNK that invokes BumbleBee via rundll32 — leading to Conti/Diavol via Wizard Spider. Most generic phishing detections fail here because (a) the URL reputation is clean (WeTransfer is

ucs.title: [LLM] EXOTIC LILY BumbleBee execution: rundll32 spawned from a mounted ISO/IMG with named-export DLL invocation
ucs.description: Catches the post-click stage of EXOTIC LILY's chain: the user double-clicks a LNK inside a mounted ISO/IMG, which executes rundll32.exe with the BumbleBee DLL on the virtual drive letter and a named export (e.g. IternalJob, SetPath). The execution from a non-fixed/removable volume mount path with a comma-export argument is highly characteristic of BumbleBee staging by EXOTIC LILY (and contrasts with normal rundll32 use by Windows itself).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND (Processes.process="*.dll,*" OR Processes.process="*.dll *") AND NOT (Processes.process="*\\Windows\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Program Files*") by Processes.user, Processes.dest, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.process_path
| rename Processes.* as *
| rex field=cmdlines "(?i)(?<drive>[D-Z]):\\\\"
| rex field=cmdlines "(?i)\.dll[\",]\s*(?<export>[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,40})"
| where isnotnull(drive) AND isnotnull(export)
| where NOT match(parent_process_name,"(?i)(msiexec|setup|installer|update)\\.exe")
| eval is_user_account=if(match(user,"\$$"),0,1)
| where is_user_account=1
| table first_seen, last_seen, user, dest, parent_process_name, process_path, drive, export, cmdlines
| sort - last_seen
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// EXOTIC LILY — BumbleBee rundll32 from mounted ISO drive (Sentinel / 4688)
let LookbackDays = 14d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith "\\regsvr32.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith "\\odbcconf.exe"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)[D-Z]:\\[^ ,\"]+\.dll[\"]?\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,40}"
| where CommandLine !contains @"C:\Windows\"
| where CommandLine !contains @"C:\Program Files"
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\explorer.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\wscript.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\cscript.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\outlook.exe"
| extend MountDrive = extract(@"(?i)([D-Z]):\\", 1, CommandLine),
         DllPath    = extract(@"(?i)([D-Z]:\\[^ ,\"]+\.dll)", 1, CommandLine),
         Export     = extract(@"(?i)\.dll[\"]?\s*,\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,40})", 1, CommandLine)
| where isnotempty(MountDrive) and isnotempty(Export)
| where Export !in~ ("PrintUIEntry","OpenAs_RunDLL","Control_RunDLL","ShellExec_RunDLL","InstallHinfSection")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          Parent = ParentProcessName, NewProcessName,
          MountDrive, DllPath, Export, CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: EXOTIC LILY BumbleBee Loader - rundll32 DLL Export from Mounted ISO
id: a4e2c1b8-7f3d-4e6a-9c1b-exotic-lily-bumblebee
status: experimental
description: Detects EXOTIC LILY (G1011) BumbleBee staging where rundll32 invokes a DLL on a non-system drive letter (D:-Z:) using the comma-export syntax. The DLL lives on a mounted ISO/IMG container delivered via the actor's file-share-link phishing chain and tied to follow-on Conti/Diavol ransomware via Wizard Spider.
references:
    - https://blog.google/threat-analysis-group/exposing-initial-access-broker-ties-conti/
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1011/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
    - attack.execution
    - attack.t1204.002
    - attack.t1218.011
    - attack.g1011
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_loader:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\rundll32.exe'
            - '\regsvr32.exe'
            - '\odbcconf.exe'
    selection_parent:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\explorer.exe'
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\wscript.exe'
            - '\cscript.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '\outlook.exe'
    selection_cmdline:
        CommandLine|re: '(?i)[D-Z]:\\[^ ,"]+\.dll"?\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,40}'
    filter_signed_paths:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'C:\Windows\'
            - 'C:\Program Files'
    filter_known_exports:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'PrintUIEntry'
            - 'OpenAs_RunDLL'
            - 'Control_RunDLL'
            - 'ShellExec_RunDLL'
            - 'InstallHinfSection'
    condition: all of selection_* and not 1 of filter_*
falsepositives:
    - Custom enterprise software shipped on USB / mapped network drives that loads via rundll32 (rare; baseline DllPath + Export pair).
    - Removable-media auto-run installers that legitimately invoke rundll32 with a DLL on D:.
level: high
ucs.rationale: BumbleBee is the loader EXOTIC LILY hand-delivers as the next stage after their file-share-link phish — Google TAG and Proofpoint both attribute its initial-access distribution to this crew. Normal user-context rundll32 invocations almost never carry a non-C: drive-letter DLL path with a comma-export argument; Windows shell context menus, control-panel applets and printer UI all live under C:\Wind

_field_issues.message: `Export` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Export` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent. Did you mean `IpPort`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
